title: cerastium alpinum var. subtriflorum
intro: Cerastium alpinum var. subtriflorum, also known as the mountain chickweed, is a flowering plant species within the carnation family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of cerastium alpinum var. subtriflorum include soothing skin irritations, wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
